rules: Always follow all requirements defined in standards/full/engineering.md.
For all Python code: enforce PEP 8 formatting, complete type hints, mypy compliance, Google-style docstrings, and structured logging with ISO timestamps.
All production Python code must include tests (pytest + pytest-cov) and target ≥95% coverage before merge.
Functions in Python should generally be under 30 lines and maintain cyclomatic complexity ≤10; if longer or more complex, explicitly justify and refactor where possible.
Never hardcode credentials, API keys, tokens, SMTP info, or secrets. Use .env or secure keyring access as described in standards/full/engineering.md.
Refactoring and new feature development must be separate commits and use Conventional Commits (feat:, fix:, refactor:, test:, docs:, chore:).
When generating or editing code in any language, prefer clarity, maintainability, and observability over cleverness.
All logging must be structured, must not silently swallow exceptions, and must surface enough context to debug in production.
Before finalizing major changes, re-check standards/full/engineering.md and explain how the change stays compliant.
